title: Ashaar Khorda
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:AshaarKhorda.jpg]] |name=Ashaar Khorda |homeworld=[[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |birth= |death=[[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]] (8), [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]''[[Star Wars: Zam Wesell]]'' |species=[[Annoo-dat Prime]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=2 purple (right pair), 2 gray (left pair) |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|He led a military coup on Annoo, and would've succeeded in overthrowing the government if the Republic hadn't interceded. And there were accusations of war crimes—''"
"''Yes, but Khorda escaped before he could be brought to trial. Now he fancies himself a freedom fighter against the Republic's tyranny.|Jedi Masters [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]|}} '''Ashaar Khorda''' was an [[Annoo-dat Prime]] revolutionary. Sometime before [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], he tried to seize control of his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]], only to have the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] end his rebellion. Khorda hated the Republic as a result. He fled the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] and began to form a group that shared his contempt. Khorda also located the [[Infant of Shaa]], an ancient artifact that was rumored to have the power to destroy a planet, although once he found it, the object was stolen from him. He hired the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] to retrieve it and, once it was back in his possession, went ahead with his plan to destroy [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]—the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the Republic and, in his view, a planet that was ripe with corruption and injustice. Khorda and his most devoted comrades made their way to Coruscant's [[Power relay/Legends|power relay]], located at the planet's core. There, they intended to destroy the Infant, creating an explosion that would consume Coruscant. However, they were [[Skirmish at Coruscant's power core|opposed]] by Fett, the huntress [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]], and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yarael Poof/Legends|Yarael Poof]]. Fett shot and [[Death/Legends|killed]] Khorda, while Poof stopped the Infant from exploding, ending the short-lived insurrection. ==Biography== {{Quote|Together we will finally strike a deathblow against the corrupt, bloated creature that is the so-called Republic.|Ashaar Khorda|}} [[File:Ashaar-khorda.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The [[General/Legends|general]] Ashaar Khorda]] The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Annoo-dat Prime]] Ashaar Khorda was a [[General/Legends|general]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]]. At some point, Khorda attempted to start a rebellion on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Due to the intervention of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the rebellion was defeated. Nonetheless, Khorda did not give up and tried to gain control of the planet with the help of military forces that he had usurped from the government. The Republic put down the insurrection and installed its own leader, and Khorda fled the planet before he could be tried for war crimes. Claiming to be a freedom fighter who battled against the corrupt and unjust Republic, Khorda assembled a band of followers that bore a similar hatred for the Republic. He also began searching for the [[Infant of Shaa]], an artifact made by the [[Seylott (species)|Seylott]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that was rumored to have incredible powers capable of even destroying a planet. Khorda had heard of it from a [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] under interrogation, and although most believed the artifact to be mere rumor, the general believed that he could use the Infant against the Republic. [[File:Infant_of_Shaa.jpg|thumb|right|170px|The [[Infant of Shaa]], highly coveted by Ashaar Khorda]] Khorda located the artifact; however, it was not in his possession long before it was stolen by a [[Unidentified Seylott|native]] of [[Seylott/Legends|Seylott]]. In [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], Khorda had one of his followers, the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Fernooda]], contact the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] and [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] to retrieve the object. Under the general's orders, Fernooda did not reveal his master's identity and claimed only that he was an art aficionado who had had the valuable piece stolen from his collection. The two [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters agreed to the job, and Fett recovered the artifact on Seylott. He returned it to Fernooda, who in turn gave it to Khorda.[[Star Wars: Jango Fett (one-shot)|''Star Wars: Jango Fett'']] With the artifact in his hands once more, the general plotted to use the artifact's legendary power to destroy a planet. However, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] learned of Khorda's plans—namely, that he planned to destroy [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], or [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. A [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] was chosen to safeguard each planet, in hopes of finding Khorda and stopping his plot. Khorda chose to use the Infant to destroy Coruscant and met with a small group of his most trusted followers in [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant's underlevels]]. The General explained to them his plans to place the Infant in the [[Power relay/Legends|power relays]] at the planet's core. The Infant was a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-imbued object and therefore contained a massive amount of [[Force energy]]; when the artifact was destroyed by the electric currents produced by the power relay, this energy would be released, causing a massive explosion that would consume the planet. Although Khorda and his group would [[Death/Legends|die]] in the process, he had no qualms about this. The destruction of Coruscant, Khorda believed, would inspire others and would "cleanse" the Republic that he believed to be so corrupt. He felt that his death was a necessary sacrifice to save [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from the Republic. [[File:ZW-Skirmish.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Khorda is killed by [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]].]] Khorda led his followers to the power relay at the planet's core and killed all the guards who had been stationed there to prevent access. The group reached the core, where Khorda took a moment to bask in his victory. It was then that they were [[Skirmish at Coruscant's power core|attacked]] by the two bounty hunters, Fett and Wesell, as well as [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Yarael Poof/Legends|Yarael Poof]], who had arrived at the relay separately from the bounty hunters. The two hunters had learned of the Infant's planet-destroying properties, and as Wesell had helped Fett retrieve it, the huntress felt guilty. She had convinced Fett to help her stop Khorda and recover the artifact, with the two following Khorda to the power relay. Upon spotting the new arrivals, Khorda ordered his men to attack them. His seven accomplices were quickly killed, however. Placing the Infant in the electric current of the power relay, Khorda then attacked Poof. The Jedi cut off his arm, and although Khorda stabbed Poof with a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]], Fett shot him dead. Poof took the Infant and, using the Force, kept it from exploding. Though the effort ultimately killed the Jedi Master, Coruscant was saved. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|In order to save the body of the Republic…we must destroy its infected brain.|Ashaar Khorda|}} [[File:TheDeathofYaraelPoof.jpg|thumb|right|190px|Khorda stabs Master Poof]] Ashaar Khorda believed himself to be a brave freedom fighter battling against the corrupt Republic. He felt that the Republic was a tyranny that needed its tainted [[Capital/Legends|capital]] to be destroyed for it to be saved. Thus, he planned to annihilate Coruscant, thereby eliminating the "infection" that existed at the highest levels of government and shaping the Republic into a noble body once more. With the "head" of the Republic—its capital world—destroyed, the body would be free, as the Annoo-dat Prime believed. Khorda dedicated his life to fighting the Republic and was willing to die for the cause. This commitment and drive came from witnessing what he saw to be the Republic forcing and imposing its unjust policies on his homeworld. Khorda was willing to kill innocents, as he stabbed and killed the [[Pilot/Legends|driver]] of the [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] that took him to meet with his group in the underlevels. The Jedi High Council considered Khorda to be a [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]], and Fett believed him to be a madman. ==Behind the scenes== Ashaar Khorda was created for the [[2002]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] [[Star Wars: Jango Fett (one-shot)|''Star Wars: Jango Fett'']] and appeared in its sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Zam Wesell]]''. He later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Jango Fett (one-shot)|''Star Wars: Jango Fett'']] {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Zam Wesell]]'' ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Ashaar Khorda}} *''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Illustrated Companion]]'' {{Imo}} *{{FactFile|100|POO 1-2|Yarael Poof}} *{{FactFile|101|WES 9-12|Zam Wesell}} *{{FactFile|106|POO 3-4|Yarael Poof}} *{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|yaraelpoof|Poof, Yarael}} *{{DB|character|zamwesell|Wesell, Zam}} {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Ashaar Khorda |es=Ashaar Khorda |nl=Ashaar Khorda |pl=Ashaar Khorda }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Khorda, Ashaar}} [[Category:Admirals and generals]] [[Category:Annoo-dat Prime]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Resistance fighters]] [[Category:Terrorists]]
